framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
action: catch-up tick after five missed sessions: the 7/02 rotation filled at the 7/06 open and mostly worked — now exit V entirely (stablecoin-derating thesis break), add ASML ahead of its 7/15 print (memory-capex anticipation) and a small CVX oil hedge, hold LLY/BAC/TSM/REGN/GILD through catalyst week
sizing: 58% deployed across 7 names (LLY 12, BAC 12, TSM 8, ASML 8, REGN 8, GILD 5, CVX 5), 42% cash into a CPI + bank-earnings week under a hawkish Fed

regime.label: narrow mega-cap leadership under a hawkish Warsh Fed — earnings season opens into CPI, with oil bid on resumed U.S.–Iran hostilities
regime.note: The missed week (7/6–7/10): S&P +1.23%, Nasdaq +1.74%, Russell 2000 -0.61% with 8 of 11 sectors down — only Energy and Technology gained; Fed June-meeting minutes read hawkish (Warsh 'focused on lowering inflation to 2%') and yields rose with oil. Samsung's record-but-below-AI-expectations print knocked semis mid-week (MU -4.7% that day), yet SK Hynix's $26.5B Nasdaq ADR debut popped +13% Friday. This week is the summer's densest catalyst cluster: CPI Tue 7/14 8:30am ET, JPM/GS/BAC/C/WFC all Tue, Warsh's first congressional testimony Tue, PPI + MS Wed, ASML pre-market Wed 7/15, retail sales + TSM Thu 7/16.

thesis: What changed since 7/06: everything queued finally traded. The rotation filled at the 7/06 open — MU exited ~1007 (vs 979.30 by Friday: right call even though SKHY's debut popped +13%, the listing bid SKHY itself, not Micron), V trimmed ~363 before the stablecoin-derating week took it to 348.97, REGN filled 657.96 and is already green, GILD limit-filled 132.55, LLY topped up ~1208, BAC ~59.14. Two decisions follow from the new tape. First, V goes to zero: the 'lowest-drama compounder' framing is dead — V is -19% from its record with Sell downgrades explicitly citing stablecoin disruption of the 2–3% fee model and DOJ/interchange overhang; a broken structural thesis doesn't deserve a 8% stub. Second, the SKHY debut is the tell for equipment: $26.5B raised — the largest U.S. share sale ever by a foreign company — earmarked for 'new factories and equipment', with the chairman telling CNBC 'demand is enormous'. That capex lands on the lithography monopoly. ASML reports pre-market Wednesday 7/15, ~10% off its high with consensus at EPS ~$7.98 (+75% y/y) but sentiment bruised by the Samsung scare and no bookings number to anchor on — I'm taking 8% into the print, limit-protected, expecting guidance commentary to reflect the memory-capex acceleration the market just watched get funded. BAC (12%) reports tomorrow pre-market (consensus $1.10 EPS vs $0.89 y/y): the position was sized for this print six ticks ago and holding through it IS the bet — a queued order can't fill before the 7/14 open anyway, so churning it now would be theater. TSM (8%) holds into Thursday's print (guided $39.0–40.2B, ~+32% y/y). LLY (12%) anchor intact: the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge is live and the stock set its 52-week high 7/07 (1249.45) before settling back — the ramp thesis is unhurt by a hawkish-Fed multiple squeeze. REGN/GILD (8/5%) stay as the valuation-floor healthcare sleeve. New 5% CVX: Energy was one of only two green sectors last week, oil is rising on resumed U.S.–Iran hostilities, and the book is otherwise long duration-sensitive healthcare into a hawkish CPI — a ~4%-yield oil major is the cheap hedge. Cash 42%: catalyst weeks reward having powder when prints miss.

evidence.point: Missed-week tape: 'The S&P 500 gained +1.23%, and the Nasdaq Composite returned +1.74%, while the Russell 2000 small-cap index finished -0.61% lower'; 'only Energy and Technology posted meaningful gains, while eight of the eleven S&P 500 sectors declined'
Fed: 'the somewhat hawkish Fed minutes helped push yields higher across most maturities'; new Chair Kevin Warsh 'focused on lowering inflation to 2%'

SK Hynix debut: 'SK Hynix rose 13% in its first day of trading on Nasdaq, closing at $168.01'; ADRs 'priced at $149, raising $26.5 billion' — 'the largest U.S. share sale ever completed by a foreign company' — for expansion 'which includes investing in new factories and equipment'; chairman: 'demand is enormous'

Semi wobble: Samsung 'reported record profit that beat estimates but fell short of the market's elevated AI expectations, triggering a semiconductor selloff'; 'Shares of Micron closed down 4.7%' and 'the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) fell more than 3%'

Catalyst week: 'JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, Citigroup and Wells Fargo report on Tuesday'; 'CPI data is scheduled for Tuesday, July 14 at 8:30 a.m. ET'; 'Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is scheduled to deliver his first testimony on monetary policy before Congress starting on Tuesday'

ASML: 'slated to report second-quarter 2026 results before the market opens on July 15, 2026'; consensus 'net sales to land between €8.4 billion and €9.0 billion with gross margin of roughly 51% to 52% and earnings per share near $7.98, representing roughly 75% growth'; 'from Q1 2026, ASML stopped publishing quarterly bookings'

V thesis break: 'Visa is down 19%, Mastercard 18% from record highs'; 'Visa and Mastercard were downgraded to Sell due to credible disruption risk from stablecoins threatening the 2-3% transaction fee model'; DOJ 'has accused both companies of using their dominant positions to maintain high merchant fees'
BAC into the print: consensus '$1.10 per share, up from 89 cents per share in the year-ago period'; Robinhood fundamentals 7/13: P/E 14.81, P/B 1.54, 52-wk high 60.825 set 2026-07-07
TSM guidance unchanged, print Thursday 7/16: 'revenue of $39.0–$40.2 billion (approximately 10% sequential and 32% year-over-year growth at midpoint), gross margin of 65.5–67.5%'

Settled 7/10 closes the targets were sized against: ASML 1797.32, CVX 176.40, V 348.97, LLY 1188.58, BAC 59.67, TSM 434.11, REGN 664.52, GILD 129.83; Robinhood fundamentals: ASML P/E 59.88 (52-wk high 1999.96 on 6/30), CVX dividend yield 3.96%, GILD yield 2.48% at P/E 17.67, REGN P/E 16.19
evidence.source: data/snapshot.json (Robinhood daily bars, settled 2026-07-10); Robinhood get_equity_fundamentals 2026-07-13

risks: Hot CPI Tuesday under a hawkish Warsh Fed re-prices the whole tape lower — 42% cash cushions but the healthcare sleeve is duration-sensitive

ASML is a P/E ~60 name reporting with no bookings disclosure: cautious guidance commentary (or a Samsung-style 'beat but not enough') can gap it down through any thesis — the 8% size and limit are the only protection, a buy limit cannot stop a post-fill drop
BAC reports before my next fill can trade: a reserve-build or NII-guide miss hits the existing 12% with no exit possible until the 7/14 open — this is accepted, sized-for risk
Oil retraces if U.S.–Iran de-escalates, taking the fresh CVX hedge down with it (CVX P/E 30.6 is not a valuation floor)
Exiting V near a 52-week-range low (348.97 vs 365.02 high set 7/06) sells weakness; if stablecoin fears deflate, the compounder rerates without me

data_examined.label: Robinhood daily OHLCV, 101 symbols through settled 2026-07-10 (XOM history gap upstream — only 7 real bars)
Analyst memory brief (holdings, realized P&L, alpha vs SPY, prior reflection)
Quant screen over the shuffled universe: 5/20/60-day momentum, RSI(14), distance from 20-day high
Robinhood fundamentals on 10 finalists (LLY BAC TSM V REGN GILD ASML CVX META GS)
SK Hynix SKHY Nasdaq debut coverage
Week-of-7/13 catalyst calendar (CPI, bank earnings, Warsh testimony)

rationale.LLY: Anchor, hold 12%. The Medicare GLP-1 Bridge is live ($50/mo, ~20M eligible [DATA: investor.lilly.com, prior tick]) and the stock printed its 52-week high 1249.45 on 7/07 [DATA: RH fundamentals] before the hawkish-minutes tape squeezed multiples. Nothing about the ramp changed; the pullback to 1188.58 is rate noise, not thesis damage [ASSUMED]. Q2 print is ~3 weeks out — the position rides the refill data into it.
rationale.BAC: Anchor, hold 12% THROUGH tomorrow's pre-market print — this is the event the weight was sized for six ticks ago. Consensus $1.10 EPS vs $0.89 y/y [DATA: stocktitan]; P/E 14.81 near a 52-week high set 7/07 [DATA: RH fundamentals] with the whole big-bank cohort reporting the same morning. Mechanically my next fill is the 7/14 open, after the release — adding or trimming now is fake precision [DATA: settle_pending fills first session after placement]. Hold means hold.
rationale.TSM: Hold 8% into Thursday's print. Company-guided $39.0–40.2B revenue, ~+32% y/y, GM 65.5–67.5% [DATA: investor.tsmc.com]. The Samsung scare was an expectations problem, not a demand problem — SKHY raising $26.5B for capacity is a demand datapoint [DATA: CNBC]. Foundry remains the contractual-demand semi leg [ASSUMED].
rationale.ASML: New 8%, the tick's anticipation bet. Event: Q2 print pre-market Wednesday 7/15 (2 tick-days). Consensus: EPS ~$7.98, +75% y/y, sales €8.4–9.0B [DATA: EBC/Zacks]. What I expect instead: guidance commentary that confirms accelerating memory/HBM capex — SK Hynix just raised $26.5B explicitly for 'new factories and equipment' with 'demand is enormous' [DATA: CNBC], and that money buys lithography. Why the market isn't positioned: the stock sits ~10% off its 6/30 high of 1999.96 [DATA: RH fundamentals], RSI 43 [DATA: screen], sentiment bruised by Samsung and blinded by ASML no longer publishing bookings [DATA: EBC]. Limit 1815 (~1% over the 1797.32 close): fill near my price or skip the chase. P/E ~60 into a print is the named risk; 8% is the size that survives being wrong.
rationale.REGN: Hold 8%. Filled 657.96 at the 7/06 open, marked 664.52 (+1.0%) [DATA: snapshot/settle sim]. The valuation-floor thesis (P/E 16.19 [DATA: RH fundamentals], ~19% off the January high) is doing its job in a narrow tape; August print is the dated catalyst.
rationale.GILD: Hold 5%. The limit did its work (filled 132.55, not the 132.88 open) but the mark is 129.83 (-2.1%) [DATA: snapshot/settle sim]. Ballast leg: P/E 17.67, 2.48% yield [DATA: RH fundamentals]. Defensive pharma is what the 8-of-11-sectors-down breadth keeps bidding [DATA: finsyn]; patience, not conviction.
rationale.CVX: New 5% oil-beta hedge. Energy was one of only two green sectors last week and oil is rising on resumed U.S.–Iran hostilities [DATA: finsyn/CNBC week-ahead], while this book is otherwise long rate-sensitive healthcare into Tuesday's CPI. A 3.96%-yield integrated major [DATA: RH fundamentals] is the boring way to own the tail. Limit 178.2. Not a valuation call — P/E 30.6 on depressed earnings [DATA: RH fundamentals]; if Iran de-escalates this comes back off.
rationale.V: EXIT to zero, market-on-open. The compounder thesis broke structurally, not cyclically: -19% from the record high, Sell downgrades naming stablecoin disruption of the 2–3% fee model, DOJ merchant-fee case, London tribunal interchange ruling [DATA: Yahoo/247wallst/tradingkey]. The 7/06 trim at ~363 already beat today's 348.97 by 4% [DATA: snapshot] — finish the job rather than hold a stub of a derating. No limit on the exit.

reflection.looking_back: Five sessions settled at once in this catch-up. The 7/02-queued rotation filled at the 7/06 open: MU exited ~1007 vs a 1119.63 entry (realized ~-10%, the book's biggest scar), V trimmed ~363, TSM trimmed ~447.8, LLY topped up ~1208 (the 1223.5 limit held), BAC added ~59.14, REGN opened a position at 657.96, GILD limit-filled at 132.55. The book went into the week ~-12.7% all-time vs SPY -0.7% (alpha ~-12pts) [DATA: analyst_memory as of 7/02] and SPY added another +1.23% over the missed week [DATA: finsyn].
worked: The MU exit beat holding: sold ~1007 at the 7/06 open, MU closed the week 979.30 (-2.8% avoided) — and the feared SKHY listing did NOT rally Micron even while SKHY itself popped +13% [DATA: snapshot, CNBC]
The V trim at ~363 front-ran the stablecoin-derating week to 348.97 (-3.9% avoided on the trimmed shares) [DATA: snapshot] — and surfaced the thesis break that now justifies the full exit
REGN's re-queued limit entry filled 657.96 and marked 664.52 (+1.0%) — the valuation-floor sleeve is the only green new money [DATA: snapshot]
Every buy carried a limit and none chased a gap: LLY filled 1208 under its 1223.5 limit, GILD filled at the 132.55 limit instead of the higher open [DATA: settle logic + snapshot]
missed: SPY +1.23% on the week; a 47%-cash book structurally cannot close a -12pt alpha hole in an up-tape — cash discipline has quietly become cash drag

GILD is -2.1% since fill and LLY -1.6% — three of five carried holdings marked red into the week's close [DATA: snapshot]; hawkish minutes hit the defensive sleeve too, which the 7/06 regime read didn't price
The 7/06 report re-queued into 'a light week' — it wasn't: Samsung's print moved semis 3%+ and Fed minutes moved yields; a five-session gap between ticks left the book blind mid-week
reflection.adjustment: Deploy into dated events instead of hoarding optionality: this tick moves cash from 47% to 42% and points the new money at named prints (ASML 7/15, alongside held BAC 7/14 and TSM 7/16) rather than screen momentum — and it cuts the one holding whose thesis broke structurally (V) instead of averaging around it.
